title: angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis
intro: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis, commonly known as Yakushima angelica, is a subspecies of the Angelica shikokiana plant, a type of flowering plant in the carrot family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving digestion. The therapeutic actions of this plant are attributed to its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic, which may help alleviate various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis include coumarins, lignans, and other polyphenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
benefits: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis has many benefits, such as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis, gout, and other joint-related conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease. Additionally, this plant has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. The antioxidants present in Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis also have anti-aging effects, improving skin health and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. By incorporating this plant into one's diet or using its extracts in a variety of applications, individuals can potentially experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
actions: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease pain in individuals suffering from arthritis, allowing them to engage in daily activities like gardening and hiking. Its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties also aid in wound healing, preventing infections, and promoting faster recovery, enabling people to return to work and spend quality time with loved ones. Additionally, the plant's antispasmodic and sedative effects help alleviate anxiety and insomnia, enabling individuals to enjoy a restful night's sleep and start their day with renewed energy and focus. By utilizing these therapeutic actions, people can improve their overall well-being and lead a more balanced and fulfilling life.
intro_study: According to "Zhongguo Zhong yao za zhi = Zhongguo zhongyao zazhi = China journal of Chinese materia medica", Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis volatile oil has been found to have beneficial effects on the uterus smooth muscle, cardiovascular system, respiratory system, central nerve system, and immune system, including inhibiting uterine contraction, depressing blood pressure, and improving immune function.
constituents: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis has many active constituents, such as volatile oils (limonene, beta-pinene, and beta-phellandrene), flavonoids (kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin), lignans (sesamin, sesamolin, and episesamin), and phenolic acids (ferulic acid and sinapic acid), which exhibit a wide range of pharmacological properties. These constituents have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to the plant's traditional use in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The medicinal constituents of Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis also have the potential to improve people's daily lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. Furthermore, the plant's constituents may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, promoting a better quality of life for individuals who suffer from these conditions.
parts: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis has many medicinal parts, such as its root, rhizome, and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine. The root and rhizome of Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis are rich in coumarins, including bergapten and umbelliferone, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The leaves of the plant contain sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, including umbelliferone and luteolin, which have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, the essential oil extracted from the leaves and seeds of Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis contains limonene, pinene, and other monoterpenes, which may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
preparations: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been used for various medicinal purposes. The tea of this plant is commonly used to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and flu, while the tincture is used to treat fever and rheumatism. The infusion is traditionally used to calm the nerves and promote relaxation. Additionally, the dried roots of the plant can be made into a decoction to help alleviate menstrual cramps and other women's health issues.
side_effects: Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, digestive issues, and skin irritation in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may lead to liver damage, interactions with medications, and an increased risk of bleeding in people with bleeding disorders. Additionally, using this plant as a treatment for certain health conditions without proper guidance can worsen existing health problems or mask underlying symptoms. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using Angelica shikokiana subsp. yakusimensis for medicinal purposes.
